message:
feat(mcp): add MCP resource layer and expand server surface for agent introspection

Major expansion of the MCP server from tool-only to tool+resource surface,
enabling agents to discover system state via standard MCP resource reads
alongside existing tool calls.

New MCP Resources (crates/wa-core/src/mcp.rs, +415 lines):
- wa://panes — pane snapshot (mirrors wa.state tool data as a readable resource)
- wa://workflows — workflow catalog with step counts, trigger types, supported
  agent types, approval/abort/destructive flags
- wa://events — recent event history from the storage database
- wa://accounts — account status from the storage database
- wa://reservations — active pane reservations from the storage database

Each resource uses ResourceHandler trait with proper definition() and read()
methods. Helper functions tool_output_as_resource() and envelope_as_resource()
bridge between tool output formats and ResourceContent.

New McpWorkflowsData/McpWorkflowItem structs expose workflow metadata:
name, description, step_count, trigger_event_types, trigger_rule_ids,
supported_agent_types, requires_pane, requires_approval, can_abort, destructive.

Supporting changes across other crates:
- crash.rs: extend incident bundle types for richer crash diagnostics
- output/renderers.rs: add renderer helpers for MCP resource formatting
- runtime.rs: expand runtime hooks for resource-triggered workflows
- storage.rs: add storage query methods consumed by resource handlers
- tailer.rs: extend tailer for resource-driven event streaming
- wa/src/mcp.rs: wire new resources into the server builder, update
  ResourceTemplate imports
- incident_bundle_tests.rs: update test expectations for new fields

Also syncs beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
